channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Greetings from Jerusalem, whose holy sites host some of the oldest colonies of nesting swifts
item.description: The scythe-winged birds have nested in the cracks of the Western Wall and other holy sites in Jerusalem for thousands of years.
item.content:encoded: The scythe-winged birds have nested in the cracks of the Western Wall and other holy sites in Jerusalem for thousands of years.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Sherlock

item.title: Trump to attend dignified transfer of fallen soldiers. And, Hegseth testifies on Iran
item.description: Trump will attend the dignified transfer of U.S. service members killed in the Middle East. And, Pete Hegseth is requesting billions from Congress to help with the rising cost of the war in Iran.
item.content:encoded:
Trump will attend the dignified transfer of U.S. service members killed in the Middle East. And, Pete Hegseth is requesting billions from Congress to help with the rising cost of the war in Iran.

(Image credit: Saul Loeb)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Her son was killed by ICE at a traffic stop. She says she's still waiting for justice
item.description: Ruben Ray Martinez, 23, was fatally shot by an ICE agent two months into President Trump's second term. His mother says her family has yet to receive justice.
item.content:encoded:
Ruben Ray Martinez, 23, was fatally shot by an ICE agent two months into President Trump's second term. His mother says her family has yet to receive justice.

(Image credit: Brenda Bazán for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: U.S. and Iranian attacks continue as tensions mount on a key waterway in the Red Sea
item.description: The attacks overshadowed diplomatic efforts in Pakistan, one of the conflict's mediators, where a senior Iranian official held talks potentially aimed at reviving a collapsed ceasefire between the United States and Iran.
item.content:encoded:
The attacks overshadowed diplomatic efforts in Pakistan, one of the conflict's mediators, where a senior Iranian official held talks potentially aimed at reviving a collapsed ceasefire between the United States and Iran.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: What forecasters expect this hurricane season, as Tropical Storm Bertha edges along
item.description: The El Niño weather pattern could limit how many Atlantic storms form this year. But unusually warm waters temperatures mean the risk of destructive storms is still high.
item.content:encoded:
The El Niño weather pattern could limit how many Atlantic storms form this year. But unusually warm waters temperatures mean the risk of destructive storms is still high.

(Image credit: NOAA via AP)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: States want to bring Medicaid behind bars. Federal changes make that harder
item.description: Funding cuts and program changes required by President Trump's big budget law are forcing states to rethink bipartisan efforts to bring Medicaid into jails and prisons.
item.content:encoded:
Funding cuts and program changes required by President Trump's big budget law are forcing states to rethink bipartisan efforts to bring Medicaid into jails and prisons.

(Image credit: Ramon Dompor for Tradeoffs)
item.dc:creator: Julie Wernau

item.title: Trump has campaigned more so far in 2026 midterms than he did at this point in 2018
item.description: Heading to Georgia on Wednesday, President Trump is campaigning more often this year than he did in the same period for 2018 and has already rallied for a dozen candidates in toss-up races.
item.content:encoded:
Heading to Georgia on Wednesday, President Trump is campaigning more often this year than he did in the same period for 2018 and has already rallied for a dozen candidates in toss-up races.

(Image credit: Roberto Schmidt)
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: Peptides get their regulatory closeup
item.description: A panel of advisers to the Food and Drug Administration will meet this week to make recommendations on whether specialized pharmacies should be allowed to make some popular wellness peptides.
item.content:encoded:
A panel of advisers to the Food and Drug Administration will meet this week to make recommendations on whether specialized pharmacies should be allowed to make some popular wellness peptides.

(Image credit: Javier Arciga)
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: Trump-backed candidates, 2020 election deniers, win Arizona primaries
item.description: The primaries set the field for what are expected to be heated contests for Congress and governor in a swing state this fall.
item.content:encoded:
The primaries set the field for what are expected to be heated contests for Congress and governor in a swing state this fall.

(Image credit: Ross D. Franklin)
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller

item.title: Mamdani says New York City can't arrest Netanyahu but the federal government should
item.description: New York Mayor Mamdani said the city would not be able to execute an arrest warrant issued by the top war-crimes court for Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu, but urged the federal government to do so.
item.content:encoded:
New York Mayor Mamdani said the city would not be able to execute an arrest warrant issued by the top war-crimes court for Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu, but urged the federal government to do so.

(Image credit: Anna Connors/Anna Connors)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
